This is due to BetterHelp's high customer acquisition costs; the company is spending the majority of its operational expenses on marketing and advertising.

Q3 Earnings results

Income Statement

TDOC's Q3 revenue was $641 million, a 3% decline compared to Q3 2023, beating analysts' estimates by $8.5 million. The decline was mainly caused by a 10% decrease in BetterHelp's revenue, while IC revenue increased by 3%, and Adjusted EBITDA decreased by 6%. Management has stated it aims to reduce their OPEX costs as a way to increase the profitability of the company operationally. The company reduced their R&D spending by 14%, SG&A by 4% and other expenses by 5%, which brought a total reduction of 3% in operational expenses and resulted in a 31% decrease in Operational losses.

For the full-year guidance, the company guided for a low to mid-single digit growth in revenue, a mid-point of 15% in EBITDA margins and a mid-point of 94 million in IC members. The company did not provide any guidance for BetterHelp since the Q2 earnings of 2024.

Cashflow Statement

Even though TDOC is operationally unprofitable, it is profitable on a cash flow basis. The reason for the operational unprofitability is the D&A charges, which accounted for more than 80% of operational cash flow in Q3 last year. However, looking at their cashflow statement we can see that the company is generating good cashflow, it generated $110 million in CFO and $108.6 million in FCF when excluding growth capex (Purchase of intangibles) and SBC. Note: How I calculated my adjusted FCF is by deducting SBC since I consider it a real expense for shareholders. I came to a figure of $45 million in adjusted FCF. When you add back SBC, Free Cash Flow to the firm is $79 million as stated by the company.

Balance Sheet

Looking at the balance sheet, the first thing to point out is the $550 million Livongo convertible notes that mature in June. The conversion price for these notes is around $70 per share ($1000 / 13.94), which is far out-of-money from the current trading price of $14.33. Most likely; these notes will not convert to equity, but I cannot assure that. Also, TDOC has enough cash to pay back these notes in case they don't convert. This is an impressive balance sheet, with $1.6 billion in current assets and $1.9 billion in non-current assets, bringing a total of $3.5 billion in assets vs. $2 billion in liabilities. It really doesn't matter what happens to these convertible notes; the balance sheet will take care of itself.

Company Valuation

TDOC remains attractive for value investors that see a beaten-down stock ready for a reversal. The FWD Price/Cashflow ratio is trading at a 39% discount while the FWD Price/Sales ratio trades at a 74% discount relative to its sector even after a 50% rally since the beginning of this year.

Upcoming Earnings call

On the 26th of Feb, the company will be announcing their Q4 and full-year 2024 earnings results. I am looking forward to hearing more details about how the company is looking to integrate Catapult Health into their existing services; they did touch on that when they announced the news, but a detailed explanation would help in trying to gauge how that could boost IC revenues. I'm also interested in learning more about the AI product they announced. Is it going to be a new product segment they'd be selling to healthcare institutions, or is it going to be infused into the existing services they offer, and if so, how will it be monetized? This will give us a clear view of what could be a growth catalyst for TDOC.

Conclusion

I personally tend to watch for other qualitative metrics, growth in this case, to develop enough confidence to warrant a buy. I am not really a growth investor or a value investor. I'm more of a... let's say, hybrid investor. I look for value opportunities with a catalyst that might result in growth, and TDOC is not giving me the confidence as of yet. The company still has to prove it has the capabilities to successfully transition BetterHelp to be a B2B service since they don't show any interest in selling this part of the business. Once I see growth in revenue, I might warrant it a buy; for now, I will give it a hold rating.
